subject: HIST
course_description: Through analysis of a range of texts, images, and historical debates, this discussion-driven seminar examines political and cultural transformations in Europe and beyond during the period of Napoleon Bonaparte¿s wars and ascendancy (1796-1815).
course_notes: Through analysis of a range of texts, images, and historical debates, this discussion-driven seminar examines Europe's political and cultural transformations during the period of Napoleon Bonaparte's ascendancy (1796-1815), developing central historical and critical skills along the way. We will explore the nature of Napoleonic rule itself, in France and in other areas of Europe (as well as Egypt and Haiti), plus such other central themes as: the rise of nationalism and romanticism in politics and culture; the experiences of mass warfare by soldiers and civilians; tensions between collaboration and resistance in occupied lands; constructions of gender; and querying the revolutionary and modernizing legacies of Napoleon, man and myth. Assessments include a short essay, two short source analyses, a book review and associated presentation, and active participation in discussion.
